note_sections.text: Patient is a 71-year-old female with Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic nephropathy and chronic kidney disease stage 3, well-managed on insulin therapy. She presents for routine follow-up. Blood pressure is controlled at 128/76 mmHg on lisinopril. She denies edema, chest pain, or shortness of breath. HbA1c was 7.1% at last visit three months ago.

1. Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus with hyperglycemia — well controlled
2. Chronic kidney disease, stage 3 — stable, creatinine 1.6
3. Hypertension — controlled
4. Long-term insulin use
5. Diabetic nephropathy

Insulin glargine 20 units at bedtime
Lisinopril 20mg daily
Atorvastatin 40mg daily
Metformin held due to CKD stage 3
BP: 128/76 mmHg, HR: 72 bpm, Weight: 74kg, BMI: 26.4, Temperature: 98.4F, SpO2: 98%
HbA1c: 7.1% (3 months ago). Creatinine: 1.6 mg/dL. eGFR: 42. Urine albumin-creatinine ratio: 180 mg/g.
